notes: Source-backed appearance audit data. Ranges are inclusive. appears = on-screen appearance or meaningful interaction/dialogue; focused = episode materially focuses on the character/faction. remote stays searchable only when the app includes remote context, and is not shown as a tooltip appearance tag.

excluded.1039: Who's-Who recounts the Red Hair Pirates stealing the Gomu Gomu no Mi during his past; dialogue context only, no visual appearance of Shanks.
excluded.1040: Who's-Who confirms the Gomu Gomu theft by Red Hair Pirates; mentioned only, no on-screen Shanks.

excluded.95: Anime-only: Usopp imagines a shadowy silhouette of Whitebeard while Ace talks about him. This is a mental visualization, not Whitebeard actually appearing on screen.
excluded.507: Episode 507: Usopp drops a newspaper showing Whitebeard's body in a photo (post-death news article). Not an on-screen anime appearance.

excluded.95: Same as Whitebeard's exclusion: Usopp imagines the crew via silhouette; no real members appear.

excluded.528-529: Episodes 528-529: 'Big Mam' epithet is shown in romanized text; Big Mom may be heard via Den Den Mushi but is not confirmed visually on-screen. Debut is Episode 571.

excluded.886: Episode 886 covers Shanks meeting Five Elders at Mariejois; no confirmed on-screen Kaido appearance in this episode.

excluded.964: Episode 964: Buggy appears in past-era as Roger's apprentice; Buggy Pirates as an organization don't yet exist in that flashback.

excluded.31: Yosaku name-mentions Mihawk and Jinbe as Warlords of the Sea  — mention only, no Warlord appears on-screen.
excluded.67: Igaram mentions Crocodile by alias as the organization's boss and as a Warlord  — mention only, no on-screen Warlord appearance.
excluded.127: Tashigi apprehends Crocodile and strips him of his Warlord title  — institutional reference within Alabasta range; no group meeting shown.

excluded.66: Igaram mentions Crocodile both by alias and as the organization's boss  — mention only.
excluded.67: Crocodile mentioned as a Warlord by Igaram  — name mention only, no on-screen appearance.

excluded.31: Yosaku name-mentions Jinbe as one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea  — name mention only, no on-screen appearance.

excluded.409: Chapter 515 reference may show Hancock on a Kuja ship  — if her face appears, this precedes the episode 410 debut. Placed in excluded pending confirmation.

excluded.337: Thriller Bark Arc begins with Brook's debut. Moria is named by an old man as the shadow thief but is not yet shown on-screen  — name mention only.
excluded.342: Moria's three zombie servants enter his room to wake him  — Moria's presence is implied but he is not yet visually on-screen in this episode per wiki citations.

excluded.23: Mihawk is first mentioned by his epithet 'Hawk Eyes'  — epithet mention only before his debut in episode 24.
excluded.31: Mihawk mentioned by Yosaku as a powerful Warlord  — name mention only, no on-screen appearance.

excluded.418: Cover story episode about Franky at Karakuri Island (the island Dr. Vegapunk was born on, where Kuma sent Franky)  — Kuma is not on-screen; only the consequence of his action is shown.
excluded.419: Cover story episode about Chopper at Torino Kingdom (where Kuma sent Chopper)  — same exclusion rationale as 418.
excluded.420: Cover story episode about Robin and Usopp  — Kuma not on-screen.

excluded.896: Non-canon Cidre Guild Arc filler episode; multiple Supernova names and wanted posters appear as background references only.

excluded.519: Post-timeskip gathering at Sabaody; Law is mentioned but the Heart Pirates themselves are not confirmed on-screen in this episode specifically.

excluded.582: Episode 582: Momonosuke mentioned by name as Kinemon's 'son' but not shown on-screen; excluded from appears.
